title: The Chartered Accountants Act, 1949
full_text: The Chartered Accountants Act, 1949 चार्टर्ड अकाउन्टेण्ट अधिनियम, 1949 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 194938 Act Number: 38 Enactment Date: 1949-05-01 Act Year: 1949 Short Title: The Chartered Accountants Act, 1949 Hindi Title: चार्टर्ड अकाउन्टेण्ट अधिनियम, 1949 Long Title: An Act to make provision for the regulation of the profession of chartered accountants. Ministry: Ministry of Corporate Affairs Enforcement Date: 01-07-1949 Notification: 1st July, 1949, vide Notification No. I OA (4)/49, dated 1st June, 1949, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(i). Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles Show all section The Chartered Accountants Act, 1949 CHAPTER I PRELIMINARY CHAPER II THE INSTITUTE OF CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS OF INDIA CHAPTER III COUNCIL OF THE INSTITUTE CHAPTER IV REGISTER OF MEMBERS CHAPTER V MISCONDUCT CHAPTER VI REGIONAL COUNCILS CHAPTER VII PENALTIES CHAPTER VII A QUALITY REVIEW BOARD CHAPTER VIII MISCELLANEOUS Section 1. Short title, extent and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Interpretation. Section 3. Incorporation of the Institute. Show Related Subordinates Section 4. Entry of names in the Register of members. Section 5. Fellows and Associates. Section 6. Certificate of practice. Section 7. Members to be known as Chartered Accountants. Section 8. Disabilities. Section 9. Constitution of the Council of the Institute Show Related Subordinates Section 9A. Coordination Committee. Section 10. Re-election or re-nomination to Council. Section 10A. Settlement of dispute regarding election. Section 10B. Establishment of Tribunal. Show Related Subordinates Section 11. Nomination in default of election or nomination. Section 12. President and Vice-President. Section 13. Resignation of membership and casual vacancies. Section 14. Duration and dissolution of Council. Section 15. Functions of the Council. Section 15A. Imparting education by Universities and other bodies. Section 15B. Functions of Institute. Section 16. Officers and employees, salary, allowances, etc. Section 17. Committees of the Council. Section 18. Finance of the Council. Section 19. Register of members. Show Related Subordinates Section 20. Removal from the Register of members. Show Related Subordinates Section 21. Disciplinary Directorate. Show Related Subordinates Section 21A. Board of Discipline. Show Related Subordinates Section 21B. Disciplinary Committee. Show Related Subordinates Section 21C. Authority, Disciplinary Committee, Board of Discipline and Director (Discipline) to have powers of civil court. Section 21D. Transitional provisions. Section 22. Professional or other misconduct defined. Section 22A. Constitution of Appellate Authority. Show Related Subordinates Section 22B. Term of office of Chairperson and members of Authority. Section 22C. Allowances and conditions of service of Chairperson and members of Authority. Show Related Subordinates Section 22D. Procedure to be regulated by Authority. Section 22E. Officers and other staff of Authority. Section 22F. Resignation and removal of Chairperson and members. Section 22G. Appeal to Authority. Section 23. Constitution and functions of Regional Councils. Section 24. Penalty for falsely claiming to be a member, etc. Section 24A. Penalty for using name of the Council, awarding degrees of chartered accountancy, etc. Section 25. Companies not to engage in accountancy. Section 26. Unqualified persons not to sign documents. Section 27. Maintenance of branch offices. Section 28. Sanction to prosecute. Section 28A. Establishment of Quality Review Board. Show Related Subordinates Section 28B. Functions of Board. Section 28C. Procedure of Board. Show Related Subordinates Section 28D. Terms and conditions of services of Chairperson and members of Board and its expenditure. Show Related Subordinates Section 29. Reciprocity. Show Related Subordinates Section 29A. Power of Central Government to make rules Show Related Subordinates Section 30. Power to make regulations. Show Related Subordinates Section 30A. Powers of Central Government to direct regulations to be made or to make or amend regulations. Section 30B. Rules, regulations and notifications to be laid before Parliament Section 30C. Power of Central Government to issue directions. Section 30D. Protection of action taken in good faith. Section 30E. Members, etc., to be public servants. Section 31. Construction of references. Section 32. Act not to affect right of accountants to practise as such in Part B States. Section 33. Repealed. Schedule 1. THE FIRST SCHEDULE. Order Appendix Forms Schedule 2. THE SECOND SCHEDULE.
